core_mechanism: a scaling-law derivation obtained by mapping a persistent, directionally-correlated driven random walk onto the equilibrium conformational statistics of a bending-stiff fluctuating line, using the persistence-length structure to extract power-law dependences on the system's control parameters
brief_reason: The abstract states the results rest on employing an analogy with semi-flexible polymers to derive scaling laws for the confinement-induced boundary excess as a function of wall distance, rod length, and propulsive force, which the simulations then confirm.
